sources.title: GPT-5.6
sources.reason: New official frontier model release with concrete developer-guide guidance, benchmark discussion, and coding-agent tool choice debate. Follow-up to prior-day pre-release signals, but materially new because the release and HN discussion are now public.

sources.title: Show HN: Getting GLM 5.2 running on my slow computer
sources.reason: Specific local-inference implementation for a 744B MoE model with concrete memory/disk/tok/s numbers; highly relevant to local AI agent and model deployment readers.

sources.title: GLM 5.2 is nearly as accurate as a human book keeper
sources.reason: Concrete applied LLM benchmark plus substantive HN critique about task scope, retrieval, liability, and human judgment in white-collar automation.

sources.title: Hy3
sources.reason: Model release/distribution discussion with OpenRouter ranking, pricing, and free-tier details useful for model-routing readers.

sources.title: EU Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0
sources.reason: Not AI-specific, but relevant to communications, cloud data, privacy, and compliance risk for agent/tool products that handle private messages or user data.

rejected.title: Train sim created by just one person is being called the best ever made
rejected.reason: Interesting indie software/game story but not concretely relevant to AI or coding-agent workflows.

rejected.title: I Changed My Name
rejected.reason: Useful general software-design reminder about names, but the available evidence is mostly identity/localization discussion and too generic for this AI-agent lane.

rejected.title: Show HN: 18 Words
rejected.reason: Popular web puzzle with product feedback, but no AI/coding-agent-specific substance.

rejected.title: A road to Lisp: Why Lisp
rejected.reason: Programming-language essay with broad discussion, but no concrete current release, tool, agent workflow, or practitioner update for this lane.

rejected.title: No leap second will be introduced at the end of December 2026
rejected.reason: Systems/timekeeping relevance is real, but this item is generic infrastructure news and not specific enough to AI/coding-agent readers compared with stronger model/tool discussions.

notes: Read input.md, context.json, all 10 raw HN story files, and the 2026-07-09/2026-07-08 report history for dedupe. Did not use selected_items.json as primary input. GPT-5.6 was retained as a meaningful follow-up because previous reports only had pre-release/X signals, while this corpus has the official HN discussion and developer-guide details.
